_note: CANON: one record per REGION_ANCHOR (src/world/worldData.js). 'culture before monsters' — each region carries an identity (professions/food/folklore/tradition/festival/architecture/humor). Empty strings = not-yet-authored (the coherence gate wants a minimum filled and ratchets up). Content + the Codex reference regions by id. Seeded 2026-07 from the live game + docs/WORLD_BIBLE.md.

identity.food: cabbage everything, roast grubs, questionable ale
identity.folklore: the Hollow Idol beneath the training yard still whispers names
identity.tradition: every dispute is settled by a scheme that makes it worse
identity.festival: the Goblin Festival; the Merchant Caravan rolls through
identity.architecture: the Warren's alleys, the Plunder Market, Highgate keep astride the ceremonial way
identity.humor: confident optimism in the face of obvious catastrophe

identity.food: camp porridge, resin-tea, and anything that grills on a splitting axe
identity.folklore: the tallest oak is said to chop back
identity.tradition: on Timber Glut eve every stump gets a hat — nobody remembers why, everybody insists
identity.festival: Timber Glut when the loggers flood the market

identity.folklore: keep a lucky frog and the river bandits pass you by
identity.tradition: ferry fare is paid in polished junk — the bogling ferrymen set the rates, and the rates are moods

identity.folklore: the big caps are believed to RECORD MEMORIES — eat one and dream a stranger's day
identity.tradition: you are what you wear; a plain hat is an insult
identity.festival: the Great Cap Festival — the fashion capital of the world
identity.architecture: hollowed toadstool halls lit from within

identity.food: grit-bread and pit-stew — everything crunches a little
identity.folklore: strike the deepest bench of the pit and it rings like a bell; old cutters swear something rings BACK
identity.tradition: a new cutter's first chisel is blunted on purpose — nothing worth carving comes easy
identity.humor: every crew swears the NEXT bench is the soft one

identity.food: miner's tack, cave-mushroom fry, and coffee you could stand a pick in
identity.folklore: the deepest shafts follow DWARF tunnels — some machines down there are still turning, and nobody knows why
identity.tradition: no goblin whistles in a dwarf tunnel — you might get an answer
identity.architecture: goblin headframes bolted onto abandoned dwarf stoneworks
identity.humor: the pebblers tell knock-knock jokes; the answer arrives next season

identity.food: lichen scraped off their own stone shoulders; the toll, when someone pays it in cheese
identity.folklore: trolls that turn to stone by day and argue about it by night
identity.tradition: the Dawn Truce — every argument stops mid-word at first light and resumes, mid-word, at dusk
identity.architecture: a gate no goblin admits to fearing
identity.humor: troll humor is one joke, four hundred years old, still in the telling — the punchline is expected any decade now

identity.folklore: someone is stealing the catch — the fishers blame the lake itself
identity.tradition: the first fish of the morning goes back in — you don't tell the lake what it owes you
identity.architecture: docks, jetties, and a fought-over lake island

identity.folklore: the Root Keepers grow their homes and won't say how old the oldest oak is
identity.tradition: you ask the wood before you take from it
identity.architecture: grown, not built — houses that are still alive

identity.folklore: a scarecrow that everyone agrees moved, and nobody agrees where
identity.architecture: outlying farmsteads and a lakeside crop belt fading into the wild

identity.folklore: rust is sacred to the boglings; the will-o-wisps lead you to the good junk (or to drown)
identity.tradition: SNAIL RACING is serious business; a lucky frog is a life partner
identity.architecture: coven huts, skull-wards, and ancient DWARF PUMPS still draining water for reasons long forgotten
identity.humor: solemn ceremony over a polished spoon

identity.folklore: the Red-Ears say Gorkholm's goblins went soft; Gorkholm says the Red-Ears went stupid
identity.architecture: a captured anvil they will not shut up about

identity.professions: tending — sweeping, lamp-lighting, moss-trimming; the sprites' only industry is care
identity.food: dew, berries, and the mushrooms that grow where the old floors were — a share is left on the chapel step, and it is always gone by morning
identity.folklore: nobody built these — the stone is cut too clean, the doors sized for something taller than a troll
identity.tradition: the sprites tend the ruin-chapel but won't say who they tend it FOR
identity.architecture: impossible dwarf stonework the forest is slowly eating
identity.humor: sprite humor is very small and very dry — they find goblins hilarious and will not say why
